$schema: Captain risk register — existential and operational risks with mitigation status

risks.title: Anthropic price hike or service interruption
risks.description: Anthropic raises Claude Max pricing or changes terms in a way that breaks the $200/mo envelope or makes the Kun engine unaffordable. Service outages could block the engine for hours/days.
risks.mitigation: Track spend in spend.jsonl (fix instrumentation). Maintain a Claude API fallback (Pay-as-you-go) and OSS LLM fallback design. Cost auto-throttle at $180/mo.

risks.title: Abdout key-person risk
risks.description: Abdout is the only full-stack engineer with deep context across all 14 repos. If he is unavailable (illness, injury, burnout, life event) for more than 2 weeks, every product's velocity drops to near-zero.
risks.mitigation: Onboard Ibrahim to second-engineer status. Document architecture (already done in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/STACK.md). Maintain Kun captain so partial autonomy is possible. Train Samia on operational basics. Long-term: hire a second senior engineer at MRR ≥ $3K.

risks.title: Sudan banking / sanctions / currency
risks.description: Sudan-based clients (King Fahad Schools first) cannot easily pay in USD. Banking sanctions, currency depreciation (SDG), and PayPal/Stripe restrictions on Sudan complicate revenue collection.
risks.mitigation: Sedon's Saudi bank + MADA + STC Pay routes Sudan customer payments through KSA. Investigate Lemfi, Wise alternatives. Investigate USDT (stablecoin) for Sudan customers as last resort.

risks.title: SSPL procurement rejection
risks.description: Large institutional buyers (universities, ministries, district offices) may reject SSPL due to legal/procurement policies that disallow it. This caps the addressable market at private schools and SMBs.
risks.mitigation: Acceptable for now — private schools are the beachhead and SSPL is the strategic posture (Counter-Positioning per Helmer). If/when district sales become a priority, evaluate dual-license offering.

risks.title: No paying customer within 6 months
risks.description: If King Fahad Schools doesn't convert and Ali's outreach yields no signed deal by 2026-11-12, runway drops below 4 months and default-dead becomes acute.
risks.mitigation: Weekly review tracks weeks_remaining. At 6-month mark, captain dispatches wartime-mode escalation to Abdout (cut all non-pilot work, all hands on conversion). At 3-month mark, hard escalation. Conversion-conversation with Ahmed Baha scheduled by 2026-06-15.

risks.title: Pilot fails silently — Ahmed Baha disengages
risks.description: King Fahad Schools pilot has been live for ~4 weeks. No structured check-in cadence. Risk of silent disengagement (he stops using the product, doesn't say why, doesn't convert).
risks.mitigation: Add weekly Ahmed check-in to Ali's allocation. Track 'last_contact' in customers.json. If days_since_contact > 14, captain dispatches an alert to Ali + Sedon.

risks.title: Sedon part-time bottleneck on Saudi ops
risks.description: Sedon at 15 hrs/week is the only Saudi-resident operator. Payment setup, bank operations, physical mail, MADA, STC Pay — all funnel through him. He is also the bridge to Ali in Sudan for customer comms.
risks.mitigation: Document Sedon's procedures so others could pick up if needed. Batch his tasks Mon/Wed/Fri (already in place). At MRR ≥ $5K, consider a second Saudi operator (or paid PEO).

risks.title: Captain dispatching errors (Apple Notes drift)
risks.description: The captain dispatches via Apple Notes (Dispatch/Captain, Dispatch/Cowork, Dispatch/Inbox). If Abdout doesn't check his iPhone for days, escalations stall. If Notes sync breaks, dispatches are lost.
risks.mitigation: 24h/72h escalation gate — captain follows up if no response. Daily pulse routine surfaces unread Inbox count. Backup channel: Slack #dispatch for critical escalations.

impact.2: Moderate (one month of work or $500-2K loss)

score_thresholds.11-15: High — captain dispatches alert if delta worsens
